paragraphs.text:
%md

# Netflix Content Analysis

**Course:** STQD6324 Data Management  
**Semester:** Semester 2, 2025/2026  
**Name:** Yang Huan  
**Matric Number:** P161796  
**Industry:** Streaming Entertainment / Media Industry  
**Dataset:** Netflix Movies and TV Shows Dataset  
**Main Tool:** Apache Zeppelin, Apache Hive and HDFS

msg.data:
Netflix Content Analysis

Course: STQD6324 Data Management
Semester: Semester 2, 2025/2026
Name: Yang Huan
Matric Number: P161796
Industry: Streaming Entertainment / Media Industry
Dataset: Netflix Movies and TV Shows Dataset
Main Tool: Apache Zeppelin, Apache Hive and HDFS

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 1.0 Introduction

Netflix is one of the major streaming platforms in the entertainment industry. It provides a large catalogue of movies and TV shows from different countries, genres, release years and rating categories. In the streaming entertainment industry, understanding content patterns is important because content variety can influence audience engagement, recommendation strategy and platform competitiveness.

This project focuses on analyzing the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset using Apache Zeppelin, HDFS and Apache Hive. The purpose of this project is to manage, clean, query, analyze and visualize the Netflix dataset in order to understand content distribution, release trends, audience rating categories and genre patterns.

### Problem Relevance

This dataset is relevant to the streaming entertainment industry because it contains important information about content type, country, rating, release year, duration and genre. These variables can help understand how a streaming platform organizes its catalogue and what types of content are more dominant.

For a streaming company, this type of analysis is useful for content planning, audience targeting and recommendation strategy. For example, analyzing content type can show whether the platform focuses more on movies or TV shows, while rating and genre analysis can help identify the main audience groups and content preferences.

msg.data:
1.0 Introduction

Netflix is one of the major streaming platforms in the entertainment industry. It provides a large catalogue of movies and TV shows from different countries, genres, release years and rating categories. In the streaming entertainment industry, understanding content patterns is important because content variety can influence audience engagement, recommendation strategy and platform competitiveness.

This project focuses on analyzing the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset using Apache Zeppelin, HDFS and Apache Hive. The purpose of this project is to manage, clean, query, analyze and visualize the Netflix dataset in order to understand content distribution, release trends, audience rating categories and genre patterns.

Problem Relevance

This dataset is relevant to the streaming entertainment industry because it contains important information about content type, country, rating, release year, duration and genre. These variables can help understand how a streaming platform organizes its catalogue and what types of content are more dominant.

For a streaming company, this type of analysis is useful for content planning, audience targeting and recommendation strategy. For example, analyzing content type can show whether the platform focuses more on movies or TV shows, while rating and genre analysis can help identify the main audience groups and content preferences.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 2.0 Dataset Source

The dataset used in this project is the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset from Kaggle.

- Dataset name: Netflix Movies and TV Shows
- File used: netflix_titles.csv
- Source: Kaggle
- Link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/shivamb/netflix-shows

The dataset contains information such as show ID, content type, title, director, cast, country, date added, release year, rating, duration, listed genre and description.

msg.data:
2.0 Dataset Source

The dataset used in this project is the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset from Kaggle.

  • Dataset name: Netflix Movies and TV Shows
  • File used: netflix_titles.csv
  • Source: Kaggle
  • Link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/shivamb/netflix-shows

The dataset contains information such as show ID, content type, title, director, cast, country, date added, release year, rating, duration, listed genre and description.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 2.1 Dataset Understanding

The dataset is structured as a CSV file, where each row represents one Netflix title. Each title can be either a movie or a TV show. The dataset contains both descriptive information and categorical variables that are useful for content analysis.

The main variables used in this project include:

- `show_id`: unique identifier for each Netflix title.
- `content_type`: indicates whether the title is a Movie or TV Show.
- `title`: name of the movie or TV show.
- `director`: director of the title.
- `cast_list`: actors or actresses involved in the title.
- `country`: country or countries related to the title.
- `date_added`: date when the title was added to Netflix.
- `release_year`: original release year of the title.
- `rating`: audience rating category.
- `duration`: movie duration or number of TV show seasons.
- `listed_in`: genre or content category.
- `description`: short description of the title.

Different variables support different types of analysis. For example, `content_type` is used to compare movies and TV shows, `country` is used to analyze geographical content distribution, `rating` is used to understand audience categories, `release_year` is used for trend analysis, and `listed_in` is used for genre analysis.

msg.data:
2.1 Dataset Understanding

The dataset is structured as a CSV file, where each row represents one Netflix title. Each title can be either a movie or a TV show. The dataset contains both descriptive information and categorical variables that are useful for content analysis.

The main variables used in this project include:

  • show_id: unique identifier for each Netflix title.
  • content_type: indicates whether the title is a Movie or TV Show.
  • title: name of the movie or TV show.
  • director: director of the title.
  • cast_list: actors or actresses involved in the title.
  • country: country or countries related to the title.
  • date_added: date when the title was added to Netflix.
  • release_year: original release year of the title.
  • rating: audience rating category.
  • duration: movie duration or number of TV show seasons.
  • listed_in: genre or content category.
  • description: short description of the title.

Different variables support different types of analysis. For example, content_type is used to compare movies and TV shows, country is used to analyze geographical content distribution, rating is used to understand audience categories, release_year is used for trend analysis, and listed_in is used for genre analysis.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 2.2 Dataset Limitations

Although the dataset is useful for analyzing Netflix content patterns, it also has several limitations.

First, some columns contain missing values, especially `director`, `cast_list` and `country`. These missing values need to be handled before analysis. Second, some titles are associated with multiple countries or multiple genres, which may affect simple counting results. Third, the dataset only represents the available Netflix catalogue in this dataset and may not fully represent the current Netflix catalogue at the time of analysis.

Therefore, data cleaning and careful interpretation are necessary before drawing conclusions from the dataset.

msg.data:
2.2 Dataset Limitations

Although the dataset is useful for analyzing Netflix content patterns, it also has several limitations.

First, some columns contain missing values, especially director, cast_list and country. These missing values need to be handled before analysis. Second, some titles are associated with multiple countries or multiple genres, which may affect simple counting results. Third, the dataset only represents the available Netflix catalogue in this dataset and may not fully represent the current Netflix catalogue at the time of analysis.

Therefore, data cleaning and careful interpretation are necessary before drawing conclusions from the dataset.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 3.0 Tools and Data Management Process

This project uses Apache Zeppelin as the notebook environment and Apache Hive as the main data management tool. The raw Netflix CSV file is stored in HDFS and then accessed through an external Hive table.

The data management process includes uploading the dataset into HDFS, creating a Hive database, creating a Hive external table, and validating the data through Hive queries.

msg.data:
3.0 Tools and Data Management Process

This project uses Apache Zeppelin as the notebook environment and Apache Hive as the main data management tool. The raw Netflix CSV file is stored in HDFS and then accessed through an external Hive table.

The data management process includes uploading the dataset into HDFS, creating a Hive database, creating a Hive external table, and validating the data through Hive queries.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 3.3 Hive Table Creation

An external Hive table was created to access the Netflix CSV file stored in HDFS. The OpenCSVSerde format was used because the dataset contains text fields with commas and quotation marks.

msg.data:
3.3 Hive Table Creation

An external Hive table was created to access the Netflix CSV file stored in HDFS. The OpenCSVSerde format was used because the dataset contains text fields with commas and quotation marks.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS netflix_titles;

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE netflix_titles (
  show_id STRING,
  content_type STRING,
  title STRING,
  director STRING,
  cast_list STRING,
  country STRING,
  date_added STRING,
  release_year INT,
  rating STRING,
  duration STRING,
  listed_in STRING,
  description STRING
)
ROW FORMAT SERDE 'org.apache.hadoop.hive.serde2.OpenCSVSerde'
WITH SERDEPROPERTIES (
  'separatorChar' = ',',
  'quoteChar' = '\"'
)
STORED AS TEXTFILE
LOCATION '/user/maria_dev/final_report/netflix'
TBLPROPERTIES ('skip.header.line.count'='1');

SHOW TABLES;
"

%md

## 4.0 Data Cleaning and Preprocessing

Before performing analysis, the dataset was checked for missing values, duplicate records and invalid values. Data cleaning is important because incomplete or duplicated records may affect the accuracy of the analysis and visualizations.

The preprocessing steps in this project include missing value checking, duplicate checking, release year range checking, missing value handling and cleaned table validation.

msg.data:
4.0 Data Cleaning and Preprocessing

Before performing analysis, the dataset was checked for missing values, duplicate records and invalid values. Data cleaning is important because incomplete or duplicated records may affect the accuracy of the analysis and visualizations.

The preprocessing steps in this project include missing value checking, duplicate checking, release year range checking, missing value handling and cleaned table validation.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 4.1 Missing Value Check

Missing values were checked before creating the cleaned table. This step helps identify incomplete fields that may affect later analysis, especially director, cast, country, date added, rating and duration.

msg.data:
4.1 Missing Value Check

Missing values were checked before creating the cleaned table. This step helps identify incomplete fields that may affect later analysis, especially director, cast, country, date added, rating and duration.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT 
  COUNT(*) AS total_records,
  SUM(CASE WHEN director IS NULL OR TRIM(director) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_director,
  SUM(CASE WHEN cast_list IS NULL OR TRIM(cast_list) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_cast,
  SUM(CASE WHEN country IS NULL OR TRIM(country) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_country,
  SUM(CASE WHEN date_added IS NULL OR TRIM(date_added) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_date_added,
  SUM(CASE WHEN rating IS NULL OR TRIM(rating) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_rating,
  SUM(CASE WHEN duration IS NULL OR TRIM(duration) = '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_duration
FROM netflix_titles;
"

### 4.2 Duplicate Check

A duplicate check was performed using `show_id` because each Netflix title should have a unique identifier. This step helps ensure that the same title record is not counted more than once during analysis.

msg.data:
4.2 Duplicate Check

A duplicate check was performed using show_id because each Netflix title should have a unique identifier. This step helps ensure that the same title record is not counted more than once during analysis.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS duplicate_show_id_count
FROM (
  SELECT show_id
  FROM netflix_titles
  GROUP BY show_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) d;
"

Duplicate Check Interpretation

The duplicate check returned 0 duplicated show_id values. This means that the dataset does not contain duplicated title records based on the unique identifier, so no duplicate records needed to be removed.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 4.3 Create Cleaned Hive Table

After checking missing values and duplicate records, a cleaned Hive table was created. Missing values in director, cast and country were replaced with `Unknown` because these fields are useful for analysis but not always available. Records with missing date added, rating or duration were removed because these fields are important for time, rating and duration analysis.

Only valid records with content type `Movie` or `TV Show` were kept in the cleaned table.

msg.data:
4.3 Create Cleaned Hive Table

After checking missing values and duplicate records, a cleaned Hive table was created. Missing values in director, cast and country were replaced with Unknown because these fields are useful for analysis but not always available. Records with missing date added, rating or duration were removed because these fields are important for time, rating and duration analysis.

Only valid records with content type Movie or TV Show were kept in the cleaned table.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS netflix_titles_cleaned;

CREATE TABLE netflix_titles_cleaned AS
SELECT DISTINCT
  show_id,
  content_type,
  title,
  CASE 
    WHEN director IS NULL OR TRIM(director) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
    ELSE director
  END AS director,
  CASE 
    WHEN cast_list IS NULL OR TRIM(cast_list) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
    ELSE cast_list
  END AS cast_list,
  CASE 
    WHEN country IS NULL OR TRIM(country) = '' THEN 'Unknown'
    ELSE country
  END AS country,
  date_added,
  release_year,
  rating,
  duration,
  listed_in,
  description
FROM netflix_titles
WHERE show_id RLIKE '^s[0-9]+$'
  AND content_type IN ('Movie', 'TV Show')
  AND title IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(title) <> ''
  AND date_added IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(date_added) <> ''
  AND rating IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(rating) <> ''
  AND duration IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(duration) <> '';

SELECT COUNT(*) AS cleaned_records
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned;
"

The release year range was checked to identify whether the cleaned dataset contains unusual year values. This is important because release year is used later for trend analysis.

The release year range was checked to identify whether the cleaned dataset contains unusual year values. This is important because release year is used later for trend analysis.

hive -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT 
  MIN(release_year) AS earliest_year,
  MAX(release_year) AS latest_year
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned;
"

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 5.0 Exploratory Data Analysis and Visualization

After data cleaning, several analytical queries were performed to understand the content patterns in the Netflix dataset. The analysis focuses on content type, country distribution, rating categories, release year trends, genre patterns and movie duration groups.

Apache Hive was used to run SQL-based analytical queries, while Apache Zeppelin was used to display the query results as tables and charts. In addition, polished versions of the visualizations were generated using Python and saved in the visualizations folder for better presentation.

msg.data:
5.0 Exploratory Data Analysis and Visualization

After data cleaning, several analytical queries were performed to understand the content patterns in the Netflix dataset. The analysis focuses on content type, country distribution, rating categories, release year trends, genre patterns and movie duration groups.

Apache Hive was used to run SQL-based analytical queries, while Apache Zeppelin was used to display the query results as tables and charts. In addition, polished versions of the visualizations were generated using Python and saved in the visualizations folder for better presentation.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 5.1 Content Type Distribution

This analysis compares the number of movies and TV shows in the cleaned Netflix dataset.

msg.data:
5.1 Content Type Distribution

This analysis compares the number of movies and TV shows in the cleaned Netflix dataset.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\ncontent_type\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT content_type, COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
GROUP BY content_type
ORDER BY total_count DESC;
" 2>/dev/null

msg.data:
content_type	total_count
Movie	6125
TV Show	2664

paragraphs.text:
%md

### Interpretation

The result shows that movies make up the majority of Netflix content, with 6,125 movie titles compared with 2,664 TV shows. This indicates that Netflix has a larger movie catalogue in this dataset. However, TV shows still represent an important part of the platform because they can attract long-term viewing through multiple seasons and episodes.

msg.data:
Interpretation

The result shows that movies make up the majority of Netflix content, with 6,125 movie titles compared with 2,664 TV shows. This indicates that Netflix has a larger movie catalogue in this dataset. However, TV shows still represent an important part of the platform because they can attract long-term viewing through multiple seasons and episodes.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 5.2 Top 10 Countries

This analysis identifies the top 10 countries with the highest number of Netflix titles in the cleaned dataset.

msg.data:
5.2 Top 10 Countries

This analysis identifies the top 10 countries with the highest number of Netflix titles in the cleaned dataset.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\ncountry\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
WHERE country <> 'Unknown'
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY total_count DESC
LIMIT 10;
" 2>/dev/null

United States	2808
India	972
United Kingdom	418
Japan	243
South Korea	199
Canada	181
Spain	145
France	124
Mexico	110
Egypt	106

paragraphs.text:
%md

### Interpretation

The result shows that the United States has the highest number of Netflix titles in the cleaned dataset, followed by India and the United Kingdom. This suggests that Netflix content is strongly concentrated in major media-producing countries. India also has a large content contribution, showing the importance of regional and international content in Netflix's catalogue.

msg.data:
Interpretation

The result shows that the United States has the highest number of Netflix titles in the cleaned dataset, followed by India and the United Kingdom. This suggests that Netflix content is strongly concentrated in major media-producing countries. India also has a large content contribution, showing the importance of regional and international content in Netflix’s catalogue.

paragraphs.text:
%md

### 5.3 Rating Distribution

This analysis examines the distribution of audience rating categories in the Netflix dataset.

msg.data:
5.3 Rating Distribution

This analysis examines the distribution of audience rating categories in the Netflix dataset.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\nrating\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT rating, COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
GROUP BY rating
ORDER BY total_count DESC;
" 2>/dev/null

### 5.4 Release Year Trend

msg.data:
5.4 Release Year Trend

This analysis shows the trend of Netflix titles based on their release year from 2000 onwards.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\nrelease_year\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT release_year, COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
WHERE release_year >= 2000
GROUP BY release_year
ORDER BY release_year;
" 2>/dev/null

%md

### Interpretation

The release year trend shows that most Netflix titles in the dataset were released after 2000, especially in the 2010s and early 2020s. This indicates that Netflix's catalogue mainly focuses on relatively recent content. Newer titles are more likely to match current audience interests and streaming market demand.

5.5 Top 10 Genres

This analysis identifies the most common genres in the cleaned Netflix dataset.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\ngenre_name\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT 
  TRIM(genre) AS genre_name,
  COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
LATERAL VIEW explode(split(listed_in, ',')) genre_table AS genre
GROUP BY TRIM(genre)
ORDER BY total_count DESC
LIMIT 10;
" 2>/dev/null

### 5.6 Movie Duration Group Analysis

This analysis groups Netflix movies into different duration categories to understand the common running time pattern.

msg.data:
5.6 Movie Duration Group Analysis

This analysis groups Netflix movies into different duration categories to understand the common running time pattern.

paragraphs.text:
%sh
echo -e "%table\nduration_group\ttotal_count"
hive -S -e "
USE netflix_final;

SELECT 
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) < 60 THEN 'Less than 60 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 60 AND 90 THEN '60-90 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 91 AND 120 THEN '91-120 min'
    ELSE 'More than 120 min'
  END AS duration_group,
  COUNT(*) AS total_count
FROM netflix_titles_cleaned
WHERE content_type = 'Movie'
  AND duration LIKE '%min'
GROUP BY 
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) < 60 THEN 'Less than 60 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 60 AND 90 THEN '60-90 min'
    WHEN CAST(regexp_extract(duration, '[0-9]+', 0) AS INT) BETWEEN 91 AND 120 THEN '91-120 min'
    ELSE 'More than 120 min'
  END
ORDER BY total_count DESC;
" 2>/dev/null

msg.data:
duration_group	total_count
91-120 min	2995
60-90 min	1532
More than 120 min	1142
Less than 60 min	456

paragraphs.text:
%md

### Interpretation

The movie duration analysis shows that most Netflix movies fall within the 91-120 minute group, followed by the 60-90 minute group. This suggests that Netflix's movie catalogue mainly follows common feature film duration patterns. This analysis is useful because movie length can affect viewing behaviour, content planning and audience preference.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 6.0 Key Findings and Discussion

Based on the analysis, several important findings can be observed from the Netflix dataset.

First, movies make up a larger proportion of the dataset than TV shows. There are 6,125 movie titles and 2,664 TV shows in the cleaned dataset. This indicates that Netflix has a stronger movie catalogue, although TV shows remain important for long-term audience engagement.

Second, the United States contributes the highest number of titles, followed by India and the United Kingdom. This shows that Netflix content is strongly concentrated in major content-producing countries, while still maintaining an international content strategy.

Third, the most common ratings are TV-MA and TV-14. This suggests that much of the Netflix catalogue is targeted at mature audiences and teenagers, while children and family-friendly ratings appear less frequently.

Fourth, most titles were released after 2000, especially between 2015 and 2020. This indicates that Netflix mainly focuses on newer and more recent content to match current audience demand.

Fifth, the genre analysis shows that International Movies, Dramas and Comedies are the dominant categories. This suggests that Netflix places strong emphasis on global entertainment content and broad audience appeal.

Sixth, the movie duration group analysis shows that most Netflix movies fall within the 91-120 minute group. This suggests that Netflix's movie catalogue mainly follows common feature film duration patterns.

From a data management perspective, the use of HDFS, Hive and Zeppelin helped organize the raw CSV dataset into a structured and repeatable workflow. HDFS was used for storage, Hive was used for database management and SQL-based analysis, while Zeppelin was used to run queries and generate visualizations. This shows how data management tools can support real-world industry data analysis.

msg.data:
6.0 Key Findings and Discussion

Based on the analysis, several important findings can be observed from the Netflix dataset.

First, movies make up a larger proportion of the dataset than TV shows. There are 6,125 movie titles and 2,664 TV shows in the cleaned dataset. This indicates that Netflix has a stronger movie catalogue, although TV shows remain important for long-term audience engagement.

Second, the United States contributes the highest number of titles, followed by India and the United Kingdom. This shows that Netflix content is strongly concentrated in major content-producing countries, while still maintaining an international content strategy.

Third, the most common ratings are TV-MA and TV-14. This suggests that much of the Netflix catalogue is targeted at mature audiences and teenagers, while children and family-friendly ratings appear less frequently.

Fourth, most titles were released after 2000, especially between 2015 and 2020. This indicates that Netflix mainly focuses on newer and more recent content to match current audience demand.

Fifth, the genre analysis shows that International Movies, Dramas and Comedies are the dominant categories. This suggests that Netflix places strong emphasis on global entertainment content and broad audience appeal.

Sixth, the movie duration group analysis shows that most Netflix movies fall within the 91-120 minute group. This suggests that Netflix's movie catalogue mainly follows common feature film duration patterns.

From a data management perspective, the use of HDFS, Hive and Zeppelin helped organize the raw CSV dataset into a structured and repeatable workflow. HDFS was used for storage, Hive was used for database management and SQL-based analysis, while Zeppelin was used to run queries and generate visualizations. This shows how data management tools can support real-world industry data analysis.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 7.0 Recommendations

Based on the findings, several recommendations can be suggested for Netflix content management.

1. Since International Movies and International TV Shows are highly represented in the dataset, Netflix should continue investing in international content to support global audience growth.

2. Since movies make up the majority of the dataset, Netflix should maintain its strong movie catalogue while also strengthening TV show content to improve long-term audience engagement.

3. Since TV-MA and TV-14 are the most common rating categories, Netflix could consider maintaining a better balance by increasing family-friendly and children’s content.

4. Since most titles were released after 2000 and many were released between 2015 and 2020, Netflix should continue investing in recent and updated content to match current audience demand.

5. Since Dramas, Comedies and International Movies are dominant genres, Netflix can use genre-based strategies to improve recommendation systems and audience targeting.

6. Since most movies fall within the 91-120 minute duration group, Netflix can consider this duration pattern when planning or acquiring movie content for general audiences.

msg.data:
7.0 Recommendations

Based on the findings, several recommendations can be suggested for Netflix content management.

  1. Since International Movies and International TV Shows are highly represented in the dataset, Netflix should continue investing in international content to support global audience growth.

  2. Since movies make up the majority of the dataset, Netflix should maintain its strong movie catalogue while also strengthening TV show content to improve long-term audience engagement.

  3. Since TV-MA and TV-14 are the most common rating categories, Netflix could consider maintaining a better balance by increasing family-friendly and children’s content.

  4. Since most titles were released after 2000 and many were released between 2015 and 2020, Netflix should continue investing in recent and updated content to match current audience demand.

  5. Since Dramas, Comedies and International Movies are dominant genres, Netflix can use genre-based strategies to improve recommendation systems and audience targeting.

  6. Since most movies fall within the 91-120 minute duration group, Netflix can consider this duration pattern when planning or acquiring movie content for general audiences.

paragraphs.text:
%md

## 8.0 Conclusion

In conclusion, this project analyzed the Netflix Movies and TV Shows dataset using Apache Zeppelin, HDFS and Apache Hive. The project covered the full data management workflow, including storing the raw dataset in HDFS, creating Hive tables, checking missing values and duplicates, creating a cleaned table, performing analytical queries and generating visualizations.

The findings show that Netflix content is dominated by movies, strongly influenced by countries such as the United States and India, and mainly targeted at teenage and mature audiences. The analysis also shows that Netflix focuses heavily on recent content and popular genres such as International Movies, Dramas and Comedies. In addition, the movie duration analysis shows that most movies follow the common 91-120 minute feature film duration range.

Overall, this project demonstrates how data management tools can be used to manage, clean, analyze and visualize real-world industry data in a structured and reproducible way. The workflow also shows the importance of combining technical data management skills with industry-based interpretation.

The analysis also shows that Netflix focuses heavily on recent content and popular genres such as International Movies, Dramas and Comedies.
